phase_notes: Minimum viable. Used for pasted prose and .txt uploads.
Minimum viable. Preferred format for structured context.
Text content extracted from HTML pages fetched via URL. Original HTML is not retained.
Python, Jupyter notebook source, configuration files. Fetched from GitHub READMEs and code files.
v1.2 fast-follow: PDF extraction is the dominant Seeder friction pattern per walkthroughs 203, 204, 205, 206. Closing this gap unlocks most academic content.
v1.2: accessibility for consultancy-style contributors who work primarily in Word.
v1.2: conference talks and vendor webinar decks contain valuable model descriptions.
v1.2: academic contributors may prefer LaTeX for equations and formal notation. Text-native but treated as a document format.
Reference parameter tables (e.g., typical service time distributions by domain).
Structured parameter sets and configuration data.
Excel workbooks with parameter tables or calibration data.
Voice recordings to be transcribed. Deferred per 027_raw_context_ingestion.md; v3 introduces the voice capture workflow.
External audio reference (podcast, interview). Transcript produced via external service.
PNG/JPG/WebP screenshots, photos, and whiteboard captures. Text extraction via OCR.
SVG diagrams. No text extraction supported; captions or descriptions only.
BPMN, flowchart, or sketch images with modeled DES logic. Text extraction via OCR where possible.
Uploaded video content with transcript. Low priority.
YouTube, Vimeo, and similar external video references. Transcripts fetched from captions when available.
